Pay attention to what this person might offer you in terms of your own growth as an individual.

element_strength.Conclusion: With a good balance of Wood in your relationship, you and your partner probably compromise easily. Understanding, empathy, and flexibility all make your love match work. Hooray! It’s likely you’re both good-hearted and generous with your love. You’re willing to bend when necessary and cooperate to make things work.

With a lot of Fire in your match, there should be plenty of charisma, excitement, and  adventure to keep you two entertained. The love sparks should be plentiful, and you'll have enough passion to keep things interesting for a long time. You two are probably original, dynamic, and innovative as individuals and lovers.

Since Earth represents stability, there is a good chance you can work through all your differences by just continuing to plod on. As a practical team, you two know what's important and place a high emphasis on staying grounded and connected.  You might be more in touch with nature than other couples as well, preferring a more simple and non-materialistic life to a wild and flashy one.

With a good balance of Metal, your match has that fighting spirit which keeps love alive! Together, you can probably persevere through your obstacles and stay driven to achieve your personal and professional goals as a team. There should be plenty of  strength and fortitude to keep your connection solid, even when things get rough.

With a good balance of Water in your match, you two should make a flexible, communicative pair. You both probably place a lot of value on your interpersonal relationships, which means your love should flourish under the close care and attention you give it. You can probably deal with changes or obstacles with ease and grace.

luck_cycle.Transition: Sometimes you are oh-so-good for each other. Then, there are times when you might wish you had never met! Isn't that how it is with every relationship?
luck_cycle.LuckSitutation: Luck can work for or against you depending upon how alert you are. Carpe diem! When it’s good, press ahead with everything you’ve got, using your relationship to your advantage. On the other hand, when it’s obvious Luck is against you, step back. Your partner may do more harm than good. Just let things take their natural course, and don’t expect your other half to get you out of that difficult situation.

luck_cycle.Transition: You might have a bit of luck, but it could run out quickly. It's probably best to rely on good communication and mutual respect instead of good fortune.
luck_cycle.LuckSitutation: You'll probably have a dose of good luck but perhaps a stronger dose of the other kind. Try to be careful about making impulsive decisions, as they could come back to bite you in the end.

luck_cycle.Transition: This match is a tad lucky, but more neutral than anything else. Your partner may not bring you a whole lot of good luck, but he probably won't bring the other kind either.
luck_cycle.LuckSitutation: Your luck in this love match will probably be neutral, with the chance of some good fortune. Use opportunities wisely and look for ways to attract more positive energy to your relationship.

luck_cycle.LuckSitutation: It's anyone's guess how things will go in this match, since your luck is completely neutral. This means your match can lead to wins as well as losses. Will you risk it or play it safe? It's entirely up to you.

element_luck.LuckDescription: This match is neutral. Neither fireworks and passion nor total devestation and destruction! You are both probably strong and stable people who can deal with anything that comes your way. However, you may need to loosen up a bit to have some fun once in a while!

Since Wood feeds Fire, your element match is one of the best there is! Your partner will give you what you need, and you’ll be fueled by his love. There should be plenty of support, encouragement, and admiration in your relationship.

Uh oh. Wood can have a destructive effect on Earth by draining it of resources and nutrients. In this match, your partner's Wood element could have a negative influence on your Earth element, creating challenges in the relationship. You might need to remain strong in order to deal with the struggles.

Metal has a negative effect on Wood. Picture an ax chopping a log to pieces. Not a pretty picture, is it?! Since your element is the one providing the destructive influence, your partner could find you a challenging person to be in a relationship with.

Good news here. Water generates Wood by helping it to grow. Like the rain falling on the roots of a tree, your Water element can be nurturing and supportive to your partner's growth and stability. He'll probably feed off your energy and, in turn, provide you with the love and support you need.

It isn't hard to see why a Wood-Fire match would create sparks! Your element directly feeds the growth and strength of your partner. He, in turn, will probably consume you with passion and energy. Together, you can be a powerful and influential force!

Two Fire personalities can be good or bad. Since your elements are the same, they don't have a constructive or destructive influence on each other. You may share similar personalities or temperaments, but you might not have the qualities to help each other grow as individuals.
An Earth-Fire match is great news for you. Since Fire generates Earth, like creating ash, your partner can probably support and sustain you in the ways that you need it.

When Metal and Fire meet, the latter will melt away the former. In this match, your partner could have a destructive effect on you by making you feel less stable. You might be somewhat helpless to resist his influence, so make sure he's the one before you settle down!

How do you put out a blaze? With a bucket of Water, of course! In this match, your element might have a stifling effect on your partner's energies. As opposite elements, you two will probably face challenges due to your different natures. Make sure you don't stamp out all his sparks.

Uh oh. Wood can have a destructive effect on Earth by draining it of resources and nutrients. In this match, your  Wood element might have a negative influence on your partner's Earth element, creating challenges in the relationship. He might need to remain strong in order to deal with the struggles.
An Earth-Fire match is great news for your partner. Since Fire generates Earth, like creating ash, your can probably support and sustain him in the ways that he needs it.

Two Earth elements are a so-so, neutral match. Both of you are probably strong and stable, but you may not be great at helping each other become better people as individuals. You might have a fair amount of stability and structure in your love, but it could be lacking in passion and energy.

A Metal-Earth match? This is positive for you! While you're probably industrious and persevering, he tends to be stable and structured in his approach to life. Since Earth produces Metal, he will probably give you the support you need to succeed and be happy.

In a Water-Earth match, your partner could have a destructive influence on you by sucking up your energies or absorbing all your love without giving back. Sound about right? Like parched soil would soak up the rain, your partner might be more of a taker than a giver. Be careful about being too accommodating or allowing yourself to be taken advantage of.

If you think of an ax chopping away at a tree, you can understand the destructive nature of a Metal-Wood match. Your partner might have the ability to influence or change you in negative ways. By nature, your Element is typically not strong enough to withstand your partner's element's force.

Your Fire Element can have a destructive affect on your partner's Metal one. Picture a flame melting away steel. Your innate heat can melt away his fortitude and stability. Since you're probably passionate and impulsive, this could clash with his tendency for structure and patience.

An Earth-Metal relationship is a positive match! Your element provides his a place to grow and form. By giving Metal the right environment to become strong and take shape, you exert a positive influence on your partner and he on you. This will probably be a supportive and positive match.

A Metal-Metal match is neither great nor horrible. You two are probably industrious, steadfast, and practical. While this relationship may not be brimming with excitement, it's also probably not dangerous or destructive. You can generally make it work since you have similar qualities.

Metal has a positive effect on Water by providing a place for it to be contained -- like a cup of water. In this relationship, your partner probably helps reign you in and brings structure to your match. In turn, you give him something to hold on to! All in all, you should be able to support each other in a positive, nurturing relationship.

Water generates Wood by helping it to grow. If you picture the rain falling on the roots of a new tree sapling, you can understand the nurturing relationship here. You give your partner an outlet for his energies, while he helps strengthen and grow yours. This should be a match where you both help and support each other. Great!

A Fire-Water match could be destructive. Since you're opposite elements, both of you have the power to destroy each other. Uh oh! Water can put out Fire, but Fire has the ability to dry up Water. Your relationship might be difficult due to your different temperaments and energies.

Uh oh. An Earth-Water match can be destructive. Since Earth tends to soak up Water, you might exert a negative or draining influence on your partner. If you take energy without giving any back, there's probably no room for a relationship to grow. Make sure there's a give and take in this match.

Metal has the ability to contain Water, giving it shape and form. In this match, your Elements support each other and can encourage stability. You probably provide the structure in the relationship, while your partner gives you something to hold on to! All in all, this should be a mutually beneficial match.

A Water-Water match is usually neutral. Since you probably have similar temperaments and personalities, it might be easy to understand one another. However, there could be a lack of passion and fireworks. It could work, but you may not have what it takes to help each other grow.
